---
title: "Regina Pascua Berba, MD, MSc, FPCP, FPSMID"
---

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
![Infectious Disease Specialist](speakers/img/regina_berba.png)

-   Dr. Berba is an infectious disease specialist, researcher, and Professor at the University of the Philippines College of Medicine. She earned her medical degree from the University of the Philippines, completed her Internal Medicine residency at the Medical College of Pennsylvania, a fellowship in Infectious Diseases, and a Master of Science in Clinical Epidemiology from the University of the Philippines Manila.
-   She previously served as Head of the Philippine General Hospital Hospital Infection Control Unit (HICU), where she led infection prevention and control strategies and helped develop COVID-19 response protocols that informed healthcare institutions nationwide.
-   She is a recognized leader in infectious diseases, antimicrobial stewardship, and healthcare epidemiology, serving in key roles within national professional organizations and advancing research, education, and policy on infection control and antimicrobial resistance. 
-   Her contributions have earned numerous distinctions, including recognition as one of The Outstanding Filipino Physicians (2022) and one of the Most Influential Filipina Women in the World (2022).

:::
